description_long: This object consists of a group of five distinct lampposts, each with a unique design, commonly found in urban environments such as streets, parks, or sidewalks. The lampposts are primarily constructed from dark, likely metallic shafts with ornate bases and midsections, supporting various lamp heads. The lamp heads feature different styles, including spherical globes, double-stacked globes, and classic lantern shapes, all with frosted or opal glass diffusers that emit a warm yellowish light. The posts exhibit subtle decorative elements such as finials, flared collars, and tiered bases, contributing to a vintage or historic aesthetic. The overall height of each lamppost varies slightly, but all are tall and slender, designed to provide overhead illumination. No signage, banners, or additional attachments are visible on any of the lampposts.
description: A group of five ornate, dark metal lampposts with varied classic lamp head designs and frosted glass globes, featuring decorative bases and finials.
description_view_0: Five lampposts are shown side by side, highlighting their differing lamp head shapes and decorative bases.
description_view_1: A single lamppost with a classic lantern-style head and a small globe finial is visible, emphasizing its tiered top and ornate shaft.
description_view_2: The lampposts are viewed from the opposite side, again showing the diversity in lamp head designs and the symmetry of their arrangement.
description_view_3: A single lamppost with a double-stacked globe lamp head is shown, focusing on its unique spherical design and slender, segmented shaft.
attribute_lamp_head_design: Classic lantern and globe styles, including single and double-stacked frosted glass globes, and Victorian-inspired lanterns with finials.
